english: Did you get the pastries, Micheál?
irish: O, a Mhíchíl, fuair tú na pastries, an bhfuair? Ar ndóigh, níor dhúirt tú tada liomsa faoi pastries.

english: You didn't mention anything about pastries.
irish: Yeah ach, faigheann muid chuile mhaidin iad.

english: Yes, but we get them every morning.
irish: B'fhéidir go gcuimhneoinn orthu murach gur chuir tú amach as an leaba mé...

english: I might have remembered them had you not got me up on my morning off.
irish: .. an t-aon mhaidin amháin a bhí ceaptha a bheith saor agam.

english: It was you who wanted to work different shifts!
irish: Nach tú a bhí ag iarraidh na shifteanna éagsúla a oibriú, nach tú?

english: It seems we don't have them. Sorry about that.
irish: Bhuel, níl siad againn is cosúil. Tá brón orm faoi sin arís. Slán.

english: So, that's 2 full Irish breakfasts and 2 coffees?
irish: So, dhá full Irish agus dhá chaifé mar sin? Cool. A Mhikey, cogar! Tá mé ag iarraidh cuimhneamh. Ar mhúch mé an cooker ar an mbealach amach ar maidin?

english: Mikey, did I switch off the cooker this morning?
irish: A Mhikey, cogar! Tá mé ag iarraidh cuimhneamh. Ar mhúch mé an cooker ar an mbealach amach ar maidin?

english: This is a great time to remember it.
irish: Jeez, anois an t-am le bheith cuimhneamh air, a Mham.

english: Don't start, I think I did but..!
irish: Ná tosaigh. Sílim gur mhúch. Má bhí tusa sa chistin nuair a bhí mise ar mo bhealach amach..!

english: You were in the kitchen as I was leaving.
irish: -Tá tú gnóthach, an bhfuil? -No, tá tú go breá.

english: -Are you busy? -No, you're fine.
irish: D'fhéadfá glaoch ar mo dhuine thíos staighre.

english: -Call the man downstairs. -He doesn't have a key.
irish: -Níl eochair aige siúd. -Yeah ach má tá an áit trí thine, feicfidh sé an deatach.

english: He'll see the smoke if the house is on fire!
irish: Bhuel, go raibh maith agat as ucht mé a chur ar mo shuimhneas!

english: Thanks for the reassurance!
irish: Bhuel, go raibh maith agat as ucht mé a chur ar mo shuimhneas!

english: Mikey, the customers are waiting.
irish: Mikey, tá custaiméirí ag fanacht ort. Ní raibh mé ach ag iarraidh rud éigin a sheiceáil le Mikey.

english: I just wanted to check something with Mikey.
irish: Ní raibh mé ach ag iarraidh rud éigin a sheiceáil le Mikey.

english: -Something on the menu? -No.
irish: -Rud éicint ar an mbiachlár, ab ea? -Ní hea.

english: -So, you haven't ordered anything? -No. I should go.
irish: So, níl tú tar éis ordú mar sin? Níl. Tá mé ag imeacht.

english: I'll see you later, Mikey.
irish: Feicfidh mé tráthnóna tú, Mikey.

english: -Pauline! -Hi, Daniel.
irish: -Pauline. -Hi Daniel. Níl raibh aon chall di a bheith mar sin.

english: She shouldn't have treated you like that.
irish: Bí á rá liom.

english: Who are you telling?
irish: Ná tabhair aird ar bith uirthi. Níl sí sin go maith inti féin.

english: Don't pay any attention to her. She's not all there.
irish: B'fhéidir go bhfuil an ceart agat.

english: -Perhaps you're right. -I'm glad I met you. Have a seat.
irish: Gabh i leith, is tráthúil gur chas mé ort. Nach suífeá?

english: Why?
irish: O, cén fáth?

english: Will you be in Ros na Rún all morning?
irish: -An mbeidh tú i Ros na Rún ar maidin? -Beidh mar a tharlaíonn sé.

english: Yes, as it happens.
irish: Ba bhreá liom labhairt leat.

english: I want to talk to you. I'm embarrassed about the other day.
irish: Tá cineál náire orm faoin lá cheana. Ní raibh sé de cheart agam..!

english: Daniel, we all have our own problems. As you've just seen!
irish: In ainm Dé, Daniel, bíonn fadhbanna againn ar fad mar a chonaic tú anois díreach.

english: I suppose so. I will be in Tigh Thaidhg at about 1pm.
irish: Bhuel, is dóigh go mbíonn. Beidh mé féin Tigh Thaidhg thart ar a haon a chlog.

english: You're on.
irish: You're on.

english: You didn't say anything to her?
irish: -So, níor dhúirt tú tada léi? -An é nach gcreideann tú mé?

english: Don't you believe me?
irish: Maith dom é anois, a Bherni, mura gcreidim tú. Maith sin.

english: Forgive me Berni if I don't!
irish: Ach tá sé thart idir mise agus Pauline agus ná bí á céasadh, maith an bhean.

english: Myself and Pauline are finished so stop tormenting her!
irish: Agus rud eile, ar aon chuma, ní bhaineann an scéal seo leatsa beag ná mór.

english: Besides, it's no concern of yours!
irish: Agus rud eile, ar aon chuma, ní bhaineann an scéal seo leatsa beag ná mór.

english: Right so. Keys, wallet..!
irish: Right so! Eochracha, wallet, condoms!

english: Condoms!
irish: A Mhack, nach tí do dheirfiúr atá tú ag dul?

english: Mack, you are going to visit your sister!
irish: Tá a fhios agam. Ach ní bheadh a fhios agat cén stuaimpín deas a chasfaí ort as seo go Béal Feirste.

english: I know but you'd never know who I'd meet between here and Belfast.
irish: -You wish! -Mobile.

english: -You wish! -Mobile.
irish: -You wish! -Mobile.

english: -Did you see my mobile? -No, I didn't.
irish: -Ní fhaca sibh mo mobile, an bhfaca? -No, ní fhaca.

english: It must be upstairs.
irish: Caithfidh gur thuas staighre atá sé.

english: That lad has such notions about himself.
irish: Okay, tá notions ag an leaid sin. Yeah ach tá píosa chraic ann, nach bhfuil?

english: -Yes, but he's good fun. -Yeah.
irish: Yeah.

english: It's weird having a lad in the house.
irish: You know, tá sé cineál weird leaid a bheith sa teach arís, nach bhfuil?

english: How?
irish: You know, tá sé cineál weird leaid a bheith sa teach arís, nach bhfuil?

english: I don't know.
irish: -Cén chaoi? -I don't know. Just like..!

english: It's strange to find a lad looking at himself in the mirror...
irish: Look, ar nós nuair a théann tú amach as do sheomra, beidh sé ina sheasamh ansin ag breathnú sa scathán.

english: ..when you come out of your bedroom. It's a big change.
irish: Tá sé..! Just, is mór an t-athrú é. Os ag caint ar athrú é, ní mhór duitse cuid de do chuid bealaí a athrú freisin.

english: Speaking of which! You need to change some of your habits.
irish: Cad atá i gceist agat?

english: What do you mean?
irish: Róise! Bíonn do chuid éadaí caite chuile áit beo agat ar fud an tí.

english: Your clothes are thrown all over the house.
irish: Níl mé mar sin, a Ríona.

english: I'm not that bad.
irish: No, níl, no. Tá tú níos measa, a Róise.

english: No you're worse, Róise.
irish: No, níl, no. Tá tú níos measa, a Róise.

english: -Dad! -Hi.
irish: -Dad! -Hiya! -Hi! -So, an bhfuil chuile shórt i gceart anseo, an bhfuil?

english: -Is everything alright here? -Yes, fine.
irish: Yeah, tá.

english: You'll soon need to put the heat on.
irish: Bhuel, beidh sé in am an teas a chur ar siúl go luath.

english: There was an airlock in the radiator in your bedroom, wasn't there?
irish: A Ríona, bhí airlock sa radiator i do sheomra, nach raibh? Yeah, bhí.

english: -Yes, there was. -Mack can fix that.
irish: Sure, beidh Mack in ann caoi a chuir ar sin. Tá sé really go maith timpeall an tí.

english: -He's very handy around the house. -Is he really?
irish: O, an bhfuil anois? -An bhfuil tú ag iarraidh cupán caifé -Yeah alright, ceann scioptha mar sin

english: -Yes, would you like a cup of coffee? -I'll have a quick one.
irish: Hey sex slaves! Breathnaigh, bhí an threesome sin go hálainn aréir ach ní chiallaíonn...

english: Hey sex slaves. That threesome last night was great...
irish: ..sé sin gur féidir libh rudaí ar an gcaoi seo a fhágáil ar mo leaba! Vince!

english: ..but you can't leave this stuff on my bed. Vince!
irish: Mack.

english: -Mack. -That was a joke!
irish: Joke, right!

english: I better go or I'll miss the train. See you later.
irish: B'fhearr domsa imeacht nó beidh mé mall don traein. Feicfidh mé sibh.

english: Would you like some toast?
irish: An íosfaidh tú ruainne toast, an n-íosfaidh? Dar m'anam go n-íosfaidh agus míle fáilte.

english: That would be great, thank you.
irish: Ach cogar mé seo leat, a Shéamuis.

english: Tell me this, Séamus.
Did you get a bump on the head recently?
irish: An bhfuil fáil go bhfuair tusa rap sa gcloigeann le gairid nó céard?

english: Me? No, why?
irish: Mise? Tuige?

english: This is the first time you've offered to make me toast...
irish: Bhuel, seo é an chéad uair a thairg tú toast domsa ó tháinig mé... ..isteach sa teach seo cois le seacht mbliana ó shin.

english: ..since I came to live in this house 7 years ago.
irish: Sin é an tuige!

english: That's why!
irish: Breathnaigh, an bhfuil tú ag iarraidh an friggin' rud nó nach bhfuil?

english: Do you want it or not?
irish: Breathnaigh, an bhfuil tú ag iarraidh an friggin' rud nó nach bhfuil?

english: Of course I want it.
irish: O cinnte, tá mé á iarraidh.

english: What's unusual is wonderful.
irish: B'annamh leis an gcat srathair a chur ar féin.

english: For the love of God. Are you trying to kill me?
irish: In ainm Dé, an bhfuil tú ag iarraidh mé a chur chun báis? Céard a chuir tú ar an toast?

english: What did you spread on the toast?
irish: Níor chuir mise tada air ach ruainne ime.

english: Butter, that's all.
irish: Níor chuir mise tada air ach ruainne ime.

english: It's a strange tasting butter. Where did you get it?
irish: Bhuel, is aisteach go deo an t-im é. Cá bhfuair tú é?

english: I'll put it this way, I found it in the most unusual place!
irish: An áit a bhfuair mise an t-im sin anois, bhuel ní bhfuair mé... ..aon im cheana ariamh ann, cuirfidh mé ar an gcaoi sin duit é.

english: Come in.
irish: Gabh isteach.

english: Hello.
irish: Bail ó Dhia oraibh.

english: Things turn up in the strangest of places!
irish: Bhuel, bhuel, bhuel, nach fánach an áit a bhfaighfeá gliomach! Mo a bhí ag inseacht an scéil dom.

english: -Mo told me what happened. -What's that, Sarah?
irish: Cén sórt scéal é seo a Sharah?

english: About the ancient butter herself and Séamus found in the bog.
irish: An scéal faoin sean im a fuair sí fhéin agus Séamus i bportach. Cén sórt sean im?

english: What do you mean, ancient butter?
irish: Im ón seansaol agus bhí brioscán breá mór ann freisin, dúirt sí liom.

english: Butter from the old days. She said there was a large amount of it.
irish: Agus sin é an aicíd a chuir tusa ar an toast domsa.

english: That's what you spread on my toast! You should be ashamed of yourself!
irish: Mura beag an náire atá ort.

english: What are you saying, Cóilín?
irish: Cad atá tú ag rá, a Chóilín?

english: He tried to kill me by putting that butter on my toast, the idiot!
irish: É sin a chuaigh agus a thriail mé a chur den tsaol, an pleota bradach. An t-im ceannan céanna a chuir sé ar an toast dom. Sé é atá mé ag rá!

english: That's what I'm telling you.
irish: An t-im ceannan céanna a chuir sé ar an toast dom. Sé é atá mé ag rá!

english: It didn't kill you. A bit of butter never harmed anyone.
irish: Ar ndóigh, níor chuir sé den tsaol tú? Níor chuir ruainne ime blas as do aon duine ariamh. Nach diabhail tráthúil nár thóg tú féin é mar sin?

english: Why didn't you have some if that's the case?
irish: As ucht Dé oraibh, éirigí as. Seo scéal mór, bíodh a fhios agaibh.

english: Stop, in the name of God. This is big news!
irish: As ucht Dé oraibh, éirigí as. Seo scéal mór, bíodh a fhios agaibh.

english: Just these two, Peadar.
irish: An péire sin, a Pheadair. Auto Trader. An bhfuil tú ag brath cur leis an fleet, a Mhack.

english: The Auto Trader. Are you thinking of adding to your fleet?
irish: Ní bheadh a fhios agat anois.

english: You wouldn't know.
irish: Ní bheadh a fhios agat anois.

english: Hello? Oh, hello.
irish: Hello. O, hello. Bhuel, tá mé ar an mbealach anois.

english: I'm on my way now.
irish: Beidh mé i Heuston thart ar a dó agus gabhfaidh mé díreach as sin go Connolly.

english: I'll be in Heuston at about 2pm and then I'll go straight to Connolly.
irish: Agus beidh mé i mBéal Feirste thart ar leath uair tar éis a cúig. Yeah, okay, slán.

english: I'll be in Belfast at about 5.30pm. Yeah okay, bye.
irish: Agus beidh mé i mBéal Feirste thart ar leath uair tar éis a cúig. Yeah, okay, slán.

english: You have a long journey ahead of you.
irish: Bye dad, tá aistear fada romhat.

english: I can take it easy on the train.
irish: Níl sé ró-fhada. Beidh mé in ann scíth a ligean ar an traein.

english: Why don't you take the car?
irish: An traein? Shílfeá go dtabharfá leat an car.

english: I'll leave the car here to make me money while I'm away.
irish: Fágfaidh mé an car anseo ag saothrú airgid dom fad agus atá mé thuas.

english: Listen to the businessman, taking it easy on the train...
irish: An gcloiseann tú an fear mór gnó? É fhéin ina shuí ar a thóin... ..sa traein agus leibide bocht éicint eile ag cur amach a thóin ag saothrú airgid leis an tacsaí anseo.

english: ..while some poor idiot earns him money driving the hackney.
irish: Sách fada a bhí mé fhéin i mo leibide, a Pheadair. Lá maith anois agat.

english: -I was that idiot for long enough.Bye -Bye. Safe home.
irish: Slán, a dheartháir, go dtuga Dia slán abhaile tú.

english: There could be a very important dig.
irish: D'fhéadfadh dig mór tábhachtach a bheith anseo, bíodh a fhios agaibh.

english: What are you talking about?
irish: Cén sort diggáil an diabhail atá ort?

english: If they find something valuable...
irish: Dá mbeadh rud ar bith ar fónamh le fáil ann, bheadh an áit brataithe le seandálaithe.

english: ..the place would be full of archaeologists.
irish: Agus d'fhéadfadh siad sin a bheith thart ar feadh na mblianta.

english: -They could be here for years. -It would be good for the area.
irish: Agus ba mhaith an scéal sin. Ba bhreá an rud é ag teacht thart san áit.

english: Do you know exactly what will happen?
irish: An bhfuil a fhios agaibh cén chaoi a bhfuil sé ach ní bheidh i ngrást..

english: Nobody will be able to gather turf next year.
irish: ..ag duine ná beithíoch dul amach ag gróigeadh móna an chéad bhliain eile.

english: I'll solve that problem.
irish: Ach cuirfidh mise caoi ar an scéal sin. Cuirfidh mise an tobán ar ais san áit chéanna a bhfuair mé é.

english: I'll put it back where I found it and that's that!
irish: -Agus ní bheidh níos mó cainte faoi. -Ní chuirfidh tú, muis, ná baol ort.

english: No, you won't! It's against the law not to inform the museum.
irish: Mar bheadh sé in aghaidh an dlí gan scéal a thabhairt don mhúsaem.

english: The museum won't be any the wiser if certain people keep their mouths shut
irish: Is beag a bheadh a fhios ag an músaem faoi dá gcoinneodh daoine áirithe a gclab mór dúnta. An mar sin é anois? Agus cé a bhí ag seasamh le do thaobh...

english: Really? Who was with you when you found the butter?
irish: ..nuair a tháinig sibh ar an sean im chéanna. Iníon an tsairsint, nach í?

english: The sergeant's daughter.
irish: ..nuair a tháinig sibh ar an sean im chéanna. Iníon an tsairsint, nach í?

english: Sarah is right. You have no choice, Séamus.
irish: Nach bhfuil a fhios agat go bhfuil an ceart ag Sarah. Níl an dara rogha agat, a Shéamuis.

english: Mo is no informer. She won't say anything. I'm sure of it.
irish: Ní aon bhrathadóir í Mo. Ní dhéarfaidh sí sin tada. Tá mé cinnte dó sin.

english: If she doesn't, I will.
irish: Muna ndéarfaidh, déarfaidh mise é mar níl mise dul ag fanacht i mo thost.

english: I won't keep quiet. It's our duty to inform the authorities.
irish: Mar tá sé de dhualgas orainn scéal a thabhairt do na húdaráis, a Shéamuis

english: This had nothing to do with you.
irish: Nach mór a bhain an scéal beag ná mór duit ach ar ndóigh, chaith tú do smut easóige a chur isteach sa scéal.

english: But you had to stick your big nose into it!
irish: Tá an ceart ag Sarah. Caithfidh tú dul agus do dhualgais a chomhlíonadh.

english: Sarah is right, you must fulfil your obligations.
irish: Tá an ceart ag Sarah. Caithfidh tú dul agus do dhualgais a chomhlíonadh.

english: -I'll have another one. -Another?
irish: -Tabhair domh ceann eile. -Ceann eile? -An bhfuil sé sin alright, an bhfuil. -Tá.

english: Is that alright?
irish: -An bhfuil sé sin alright, an bhfuil. -Tá.

english: You're knocking them back, that's all.
irish: Tá tú ag ól go gasta, sin uilig.

english: All the better for you.
irish: Agus nach sin más fearr duitse?

english: It's the same old story for lone parents.
irish: Sén sean-scéal é, nach é, ag tuismitheoirí singil. Bíonn an oiread deacrachtaí ag gasúir glacadh le duine nua i do shaol.

english: Children have such problems accepting someone new in your life.
irish: Tá mé in ann deileáil leis sin.

english: I can cope with that.
irish: Ach seafóid ó iar-bhean chéile fear nach bhfuilim fiú ag dul amach leis níos mó?

english: But abuse from the ex-wife of a man whom I'm no longer seeing.
irish: Tá daoine mar sin ann. Ite le héad.

english: There are people like that. They're jealousy-ridden.
irish: -Ní maith liom a bheith gránna ach..! -Tá Berni mar sin.

english: -I don't like to be mean but..! -Berni's like that?
irish: Tá, dáiríre.

english: Yes. Perhaps Micheál wasn't ready for a relationship but..!
irish: Seans nach raibh Micheál réidh le haghaidh caidrimh eile ach..!

english: She took it as a personal insult that he had a new woman.
irish: Ach ghlac sise mar mhasla pearsanta é go raibh duine eile aige. Is dóigh liom é.

english: I think so.
irish: Yeah. Bhíodh Nancy an-chráite inti fhéin ar an gcaoi sin chomh maith.

english: Nancy used to torment herself about that too.
irish: I gcónaí do mo cheistiú faoi dhaoine a mbínn ag castáil leo sa jab.

english: She was always questioning me about people I met through work.
irish: Agus ní ag iarraidh a bheith ag léiriú spéise i do shaol a bhí sí, meas tú?

english: Maybe she was just interested in your life?
irish: Bhí sé i bhfad níos obsessive ná sin.

english: She was much more obsessive than that.
irish: Bhí sé i bhfad níos obsessive ná sin.

english: She used to call me all the time...
irish: Bhíodh sí ar an mobile i gcónaí ag fiafrú cá raibh mé, cé a bhí liom.

english: ...wanting to know where I was, who I was with.
irish: Dá gcasfainn an fón as ansin le haghaidh cruinniú tabhachtach...

english: If I switched off the phone for a meeting she'd be enraged.
irish: ..nó rud éicint, bhíodh sí le ceangal nuair a thiocfainn abhaile.

english: -That's terrible. -Yes.
irish: -Bhí sé sin uafásach. -Bhí.

english: I don't know how I didn't realise it before we got married.
irish: Ní thuigim cén chaoi nár aithnigh mé uirthi é sula phós muid.

english: We were very close at the start.
irish: Bhí muid an-mhór le chéile i dtosach.

english: Listen to me talking nonsense again. I'm sorry.
irish: Éist liomsa ag bladderáil arís. Tá brón orm. Ná bíodh. Caithfidh tú na rudaí seo a rá le duine éigin.

english: Don't be. You have to confide in someone.
irish: Ná bíodh. Caithfidh tú na rudaí seo a rá le duine éigin.

english: Do you want something?
irish: An bhfuil rud éicint uait, a bhean? Do chuartú a bhí mé. Trí agus ceathair ansin.

english: I was looking for you.
irish: Do chuartú a bhí mé. Trí agus ceathair ansin.

english: Three and four there.
irish: Cluiche do dhuine aonair é seo.

english: This is a game to be played alone.
irish: Cluiche do dhuine aonair é seo.

english: You'll be alone at your party too if we don't get a move on.
irish: Agus beidh tú leat féin ag an gcóisir feasta muna gcuireann muid cuma orainn féin.

english: And so what if I am?
irish: Agus má bhíonn féin?

english: Look, there is a lot of work involved.
irish: Amharc, tá go leor oibre ag baint leis. Glacfaidh muid le bheith a phleanáil go breá gasta.

english: I thought our plan would work.
irish: Agus meas tú cad a bheas dá bharr againn?

english: How will it benefit us?
irish: Ag tarraingt sclaibeirí agus súmairí an bhaile inár mullach.

english: Bringing the village's scroungers and spongers in.
irish: Beidh siad ansin i gcaitheamh na hoíche ag ceol, ag amhrán...

english: Having them there all night long singing and looking for free beer.
irish: ..agus cuartú portair in aisce.

english: I thought you were happy to go ahead with this a few days ago?
irish: Ach shíl mé go raibh tú sásta go leor dul ar aghaidh leis cúpla lá ó shin? Bhí ach níl anois. Déan dearmad air, a bhean. Ná fhéadfainn a bheith ag botheráil leis.

english: Not anymore. So forget it. We needn't bother with it.
irish: Bhí ach níl anois. Déan dearmad air, a bhean. Ná fhéadfainn a bheith ag botheráil leis.

english: And E1.20. Thanks.
irish: Agus euro fiche. Go raibh maith agat. 21st atá i gceist, an ea?

english: Is it a 21st?
irish: Ní hea. Leathchéad atá an duin seo.

english: No. This person is 50.
irish: Níl de dhith ach bia do bhois. Chicken goujons, paté, brioscaí. Tá a fhios agat féin.

english: All we need is finger food. Chicken goujons, paté and crackers.
irish: Níl de dhith ach bia do bhois. Chicken goujons, paté, brioscaí. Tá a fhios agat féin.

english: -You know yourself. -Will it be held this weekend?
irish: Agus ar an deireadh seachtaine a bheidh sé?

english: That's the problem. It's tomorrow night. I'm in a fix.
irish: Well, sin é an rud. San oíche amárach a bheidh sé ann. -Tá mé cineál i dtrioblóid. -Oíche amárach?

english: Tomorrow night?
irish: Tá a fhios agam. Bheinn iontach buíoch díot agus dhíolfainn go maith tú.

english: I know but I'd be very grateful. I'd pay you well.
irish: Angela, tuigeann muid é sin ach tá muid an-chruógach anseo muid fhéin, Jack imithe uainn agus chuile shórt.

english: We know but we're very busy what with Jack gone and everything.
irish: Angela, tuigeann muid é sin ach tá muid an-chruógach anseo muid fhéin, Jack imithe uainn agus chuile shórt.

english: There wouldn't be that much involved.
irish: Ní bheadh an oiread sin uilig i gceist.

english: -I suppose not. -I'm sorry Angela..!
irish: -Yeah. Ní bheadh, seans. -Tá brón orainn anois, Angela.

english: Excuse me Micheál but Berni and I can sort this out ourselves.
irish: Gabh mo leithscéal, a Mhichíl, ach sílim gur dtig liomsa féin agus Berni é seo a shocrú eadrainn féin.

english: -I'll do it. -But Berni..!
irish: -Breathnaigh, déanfaidh mé é. -Ach ní fhéadfaidh..!

english: Micheál, relax! I'll be doing all the work, not you!
irish: A Mhichíl, relax, maith an fear. Is mise a bheas ag déanamh na hoibre uilig agus ní tusa.

english: What type of budget did you have in mind?
irish: Anois, Angela, cén cineál buiséid a bhí i gceist agat?

english: You make out a list and we can come to some arrangement.
irish: Déan tusa amach liosta agus tiocfaidh muid ar shocrú.

english: We'll be coming to you half an hour into the programme.
irish: Right. So, beidh muid ag teacht chugat thart ar leathuair isteach sa chlár nó mar sin de.

english: Great, goodbye Mairéad.
irish: Iontach. Iontach. Slán, a Mhairéad.

english: Is this a radio station or a train station?
irish: An staisiún raidió nó staisiún traenach é seo? Tá brón orm, a Chaitríona.

english: I'm sorry Caitríona.
irish: Tá brón orm, a Chaitríona.

english: The lock is broken in my car and I was afraid to leave them in it.
irish: Tá an glas briste i mboot mo charr agus bhí leisce orm iad a fhágáil thíos ann. Agus tá mé gan dídean faoi láthair is baolach.

english: And I'm homeless at the moment as well.
irish: Cén fáth?

english: Why?
irish: Cén fáth?

english: Because of my landlord.
irish: Bastard de thiarna talún. Dúirt sé go bhfuil sé ag díol an tí.

english: He said he's selling the house.
irish: Agus an bhfuil?

english: Is he?
irish: Sílim go bhfuil níos mó bainte aige leis an gcaoi a dhiúltaigh mé...

english: I think it has more to do with the fact...
irish: Sílim go bhfuil níos mó bainte aige leis an gcaoi a dhiúltaigh mé...

english: ..that I refused to pay E300 extra a month.
irish: ..ardú trí chéad sa mhí a íoc leis le gairid. -Tá sé sin uafásach. -Tá.

english: That's terrible.
irish: So, seo mé.

english: Yes, so here I am with my house on my back.
irish: Le mo thigín ar mo dhroim! Ah, Rory bocht.

english: Poor Rory!
irish: Ah, Rory bocht.

english: Honestly Dad, I don't know what you saw in her in the first place.
irish: Honestly Dad, níl a fhios agam cad a chonaic tú inti an chéad lá riamh. Ní raibh style ar bith aici.

english: She had no style.
irish: Tá an 20/20 vision iontach agus tú ag breathnú siar!

english: 20/20 vision is a wonderful thing!
irish: Tá an 20/20 vision iontach agus tú ag breathnú siar!

english: Even so. It would do you good to get rid of this stuff.
irish: Mar sin féin, déanfaidh sé maitheas duit fáil réidh leis an stuif seo.

english: It's bad feng shui to have it lying around.
irish: Droch feng shui atá ann é a bheith ina luí thart. Ná habair liom go gcreideann tú sna pisreoga sin!

english: Don't tell me you believe in that claptrap?
irish: Ní chreidim i ndáirire.

english: No, I don't. Janet believes in it, though.
irish: Is í Janet an cailín don chineál sin, cibé cá bhfuil sí.

english: Wherever she is.
irish: Ar éirigh leat teagmháil a dhéanamh léi, ar éirigh?

english: You haven't been able to contact her?
irish: Níor éirigh. Iontas na n-iontas.

english: No, incredibly I haven't.
irish: Níor éirigh. Iontas na n-iontas.

english: Do you think she is avoiding you?
irish: Ceapann tú go bhfuil sí do do sheachaint.

english: I don't think she is. I know she is.
irish: Ní cheapaim é, a Dhaid. Tá a fhios agam go bhfuil.

english: I hoped that she would answer your calls.
irish: Bhí súil agam go bhfreagródh sí do chuid glaochanna.

english: I don't know why you hope. She does it to me all the time.
irish: Ní thuigim cén fáth go raibh. Déanann sí an rud céanna liomsa i gconaí. An bhfuil tú alright?

english: Are you alright?
irish: An bhfuil tú alright?

english: Yeah, I am.
irish: Yeah, tá.

english: Sometimes I get a bit dizzy.
irish: Tagann mearbhall beag oram scaití.

english: You should lie down for a while, Dad.
irish: Ba cheart duit luí síos tamall, a Dhaid.

english: Maybe I will. For an hour or so.
irish: Yeah. B'fhéidir go luífinn. Ar feadh uair a chloig nó mar sin.

english: You're alright.
irish: Tá tú ceart.

english: Janet, this is Suzy, your sister!
irish: Janet. Seo Suzy, do dheirfiúr. Tá mé bréan a bheith ag glaoch gan freagra.

english: I'm fed up of calling you and not getting a reply.
irish: Cuir glaoch orm in arasán Dhaid i Ros na Rún. Tá sé práinneach.

english: Call me, now! I'm in Dad's apartment in Ros na Rún. It's urgent.
irish: Seo í an uimhir ar eagla go mbeadh sí dearmadtha agat. 091..!

english: In case you've forgotten the number is 091..!
irish: Seo í an uimhir ar eagla go mbeadh sí dearmadtha agat. 091..!

english: So, I don't love you?
irish: So, níl aon ghrá agam duit?

english: No.
irish: Níl.

english: Evan, what have I done wrong?
irish: A dhiabhail, a Evan. Ach cad atá déanta anois agam ort?

english: Is that the reason Micheál has been scratched out of this picture?
irish: Gabh i leith. An b'shin é an fáth a bhfuil Micheál scriosta amach as an bpictiúr ar fad agat?

english: Yes.
irish: Sea. Ach a Evan, a stóirín, is uafásach an rud é sin le déanamh?

english: But Evan that's a terrible thing to do.
irish: Ach a Evan, a stóirín, is uafásach an rud é sin le déanamh?

english: Listen to me now.
irish: Gabh i leith nóiméad go dtí mise anois.

english: -Who told you that I didn't love you? -Mammy.
irish: Cé a dúirt leat nach raibh aon ghrá ag Micheál duit? Mama.

english: Mammy?
irish: Mama.

english: So Mammy told you that I didn't love you?
irish: So, dúirt Mama le hEvan nach raibh aon ghrá ag Micheál dó.

english: Berni, come here.
irish: -A Bherni, gabh i leith nóiméad.- -Nach bhfeiceann tú go bhfuil mé cruógach?

english: -I'm busy. -What have you been telling Evan?
irish: Cad é seo atá tusa a rá fumsa le hEvan?

english: Shh, the customers might hear you.
irish: -Cloisfidh na custaiméirí tú. -Ar dhúirt tú nach raibh aon ghrá agam dó?

english: -Did you tell him I didn't love him? -Now is not the time.
irish: A Mhichíl, ní hé seo an t-am.

english: Perhaps not. I'll be back at 5 pm when it's quiet...
irish: B'fhéidir nach é ach beidh mise ar ais anseo ag a cúig nuair atá rudaí ciúin.

english: ..to discuss this. Do you understand?
irish: Agus tá mé ag iarraidh é seo ar fad a phlé. An dtuigeann tú mé?

english: Alright! Alright!
irish: Alright. Alright.

english: I like the idea!
irish: Is breá liomsa é mar phlean.

english: You haven't said much, Caitríona. Are you happy with it?
irish: Níl mórán ráite agatsa a Chaitríona. Sásta?

english: It's not that I'm against hosting a programme with Rory..!
irish: Bhuel ní hé go bhfuil tada agamsa in aghaidh clár a chur i láthair le Rory. -Tá súil agam nach bhfuil. -Tá a fhios agat go maith nach bhfuil

english: -I should hope not. -You know well I'm not.
irish: -Tá súil agam nach bhfuil. -Tá a fhios agat go maith nach bhfuil

english: -But..! -But what?
irish: -Ach! -Ach?

english: I don't think it's very practical in a small station like this.
irish: Bhuel ní dóigh liom gur socrú an-phraicticiúl é do stáisiún chomh beag leis seo. Is ar éigean atá an dóthain foirne faoi láthair leis an sceideal atá againn a choinneáil ar aer.

english: We barely have enough people to put out the current schedule.
irish: Gan trácht ar a bheith ag dúbailt suas mar seo.

english: Not to mention doubling up like this.
irish: Gan trácht ar a bheith ag dúbailt suas mar seo.

english: Well, we need a central focus within the schedule.
irish: Bhuel teastaíonn fócas láidir don sceideal mar sin féin.

english: I think the two of you together can do that.
irish: Agus creidim go bhféadfadh sibhse é a dhéanamh le chéile.

english: -It's worth a try. -Sure.
irish: -Is fiú triail a bhaint as cinnte. -Siúráilte.

english: That's that then.
irish: Tá sé socraithe mar sin. Ceart go leor caithfidh mise greadadh liom.

english: I need to make a move.
irish: Tá sé socraithe mar sin. Ceart go leor caithfidh mise greadadh liom.

english: I'll speak to you both tomorrow.
irish: Labhróidh mé libh amárach.

english: Mickey, we'll be closing as soon as they leave.
irish: Mikey, beidh muid ag dúnadh anois nuair a imíonn an dream sin.

english: I've just made a fresh pot of coffee.
irish: -Ach tá pota iomlán caifé díreach déanta agam. -Yeah bhuel is cuma.

english: -It doesn't matter. -Are you serious?
irish: An bhfuil tú i ndáiríre?

english: Do I look like I'm joking? Throw out that coffee and clean the machine.
irish: An mbreathnaím ar nós duine atá ag magadh? Caith amach an caifé sin agus glan an meaisín.

english: You're the boss.
irish: Bhuel is tusa an boss.

english: Janet. This is Suzy.
irish: Janet! Seo Suzy.

english: Did you not get my messages?
irish: Nach bhfuair tú mo theachtaireacht?

english: That's very strange.
irish: Nach ait é sin anois?

english: You haven't paid your family much attention recently, have you?
irish: Ach níl mórán aird agat ar do mhuintir le tamall an bhfuil?

english: You know well what I mean by that.
irish: Tá a fhios agat go maith céard atá i gceist agam leis sin.

english: Dad says you haven't called him in 6 months.
irish: Deir Daid nár ghlaoigh tú air le sé mhí anuas.

english: Don't you know how sick he was?
irish: Nach dtuigeann tú cé chomh tinn agus a bhí sé?

english: You visited him while he was in a coma.
irish: Thug tú cuairt air agus é sa choma. Agus cá bhfuil tú anois? Tá sé ar dialysis in ainm Dé.

english: Where are you now? He is on dialysis.
irish: Agus cá bhfuil tú anois? Tá sé ar dialysis in ainm Dé.

english: Yes, dialysis.
irish: Sea dialysis.

english: His kidneys are badly damaged. He nearly died.
irish: Tá a chuid duán scriosta. Is beag nár cailleadh é.

english: No, he's not here now. He's at work.
irish: No, níl sé anseo anois. Tá sé amuigh ag obair.

english: He's not looking after himself at all.
irish: Níl sé ag tabhairt aon aire dhó fhéin. Ach is léir nach bhfuil tusa róbhuartha faoi sin.

english: But you're obviously not very concerned.
irish: Tá sé anseo anois.

english: He's just arrived. I don't want to upset him. Just call him okay?
irish: Níl mé ag iarridh cur as dó. Just glaoigh air okay? Slán.

english: Bye.
irish: Slán.

english: -Hi. -Hi love.
irish: -Hi. -Hi a stór.

english: Who were you talking to?
irish: Cé a bhí ar an bhfón?

english: -Janet! -Janet?
irish: -Janet! -Janet, í féin a ghlaoigh?

english: -Did she call? -No.
irish: Ní hea. Mise a chuir glaoch uirthi siúd.

english: I called her.
irish: Ní hea. Mise a chuir glaoch uirthi siúd.

english: But she said she'd be in contact soon.
irish: Ach dúirt sí go mbeadh sí i dteagmháil leat go luath. Cé chomh luath?

english: How soon?
irish: Tá a fhios agat Janet.

english: You know Janet.
irish: Tá a fhios agat Janet.

english: The party is sorted now.
irish: So beidh muid i gceart don pharty cíbé. Tá Berni ag dul ag déanamh traidhfil bia dom.

english: Berni is preparing the food.
irish: Traidhfil bia! O a dhiabhal beimid ceart go leor má tá bean »rann ar bord againn.

english: We're okay if the woman from Aran is helping us!
irish: Traidhfil bia! O a dhiabhal beimid ceart go leor má tá bean »rann ar bord againn.

english: I won't be under pressure. I can enjoy the occasion.
irish: Bhuel ní bheidh mise faoi bhrú i rith na hoíche. Beidh mé ábalta taithneamh a bhaint as an ócáid.

english: I thought I was supposed to be the one to enjoy myself.
irish: Cheap mé gur mise a bhí ceaptha taithneamh a bhaint as an ócáid.

english: Hi Angela, how are things?
irish: Hi Angela, cén chaoi a bhfuil cúrsaí?

english: I don't know why I bother sometimes.
irish: -Níl a fhios agam cad chuige a dhéanaim an iarracht. -Conas?

english: What do you mean?
irish: -Níl suim ar bith ag Tadhg sa pharty seo. -Nach bhfuil?

english: -Tadhg has no interest in this party. -Doesn't he?
irish: Ag peataíocht atá sé.

english: -He is being childish. -That's men for you.
irish: -Sin iad na fir agat. -Sin m'fhearsa agat cibé.

english: My man anyway.
irish: D'fhéadfadh mise labhairt leis dá mba mhaith leat.

english: I could have a word with him if you like?
irish: Bhuel b'fhéidir go n-éistfidh sé le duine ar bith eile taobh amuigh díomsa.

english: He might listen to someone other than me.
irish: Bhuel b'fhéidir go n-éistfidh sé le duine ar bith eile taobh amuigh díomsa.

english: It's worth a try. I'd say he just needs a bit of flattery.
irish: Is fiú é a thriail. Níl uaidh ach beagán plámais déarfainn.

english: Hi Tadhg.
irish: Hi Tadhg.

english: Suzy, it's you.
irish: Suzy, tú féin atá ann.

english: Berni, just the woman I was looking for.
irish: Ah a Bherni! Díreach an bhean a bhí uaim.

english: Why?
irish: -Cén fáth? -Creidim go raibh tú ag smaoineamh ar an bhia.

english: Did you have any ideas about the food?
irish: Yeah bhuel bhí Angela beagáinín ach leis an bhfírinne a rá Angela...

english: Yes, a few. To tell you the truth, I came in for a drink.
irish: ..isteach anseo le haghaidh deoch a tháinig mé teastaíonn sé uaim tar éis an lá a bhí agam.

english: I need it after the day I've had.
irish: ..isteach anseo le haghaidh deoch a tháinig mé teastaíonn sé uaim tar éis an lá a bhí agam.

english: Of course. What will you have? It's on the house.
irish: Cinnte, cad é a bheidh agat, ar an dteach! -Bhuel beidh G agus T agam mar sin más é do thoil é. -Right.

english: -I'll have a G&T then, please. -Right.
irish: -Bhuel beidh G agus T agam mar sin más é do thoil é. -Right.

english: Berni! Berni!
irish: A Bherni, Berni!

english: Sorry, we're closed.
irish: Gabh mo leithscéal, táimid dúnta.

english: I'm sorry.
irish: Tá brón orm a stór.

english: Don't worry Tadhg. It'll be a great night!
irish: Ná bíodh imní ort a Thaidhg. Beidh an-oíche ann. Meas tú anois?

english: Do you think so?
irish: O beidh agus má deirimse go mbeidh an-oíche ann sin a bheidh ann.

english: Yes. If I say it'll be a good night, it will be.
irish: Shílfeá go bhfuil tú thar a bheith cinnte díot féin.

english: You seem very sure.
irish: Shílfeá go bhfuil tú thar a bheith cinnte díot féin.

english: I always keep my word, you'll see.
irish: Agus cuirfidh mé beart le briathair. Feicfidh tú féin.

english: That could be very interesting.
irish: O a dhiabhail. D'fhéadfadh seisean a bheith thar a bheith suimiúil.

english: Did he tell you about the butter?
irish: Ar inis sé daoibh faoin im? No, ná habair gur ag déanamh ime atá tú anois a Shéamuis, an bhfuil?

english: No. Don't tell me you're making butter, Séamus.
irish: No, ná habair gur ag déanamh ime atá tú anois a Shéamuis, an bhfuil?

english: For the love of God, you can't even relax in the pub now.
irish: Dia á réiteach, ní féidir aon mhaith suaimhneas a fháil sa friggin pub féin anois. Ara cén sórt suaimhnis atá uait agus scéal breá stairiúil ar leic an dorais againn?

english: Relax? And such an historic story on our doorstep.
irish: Ara cén sórt suaimhnis atá uait agus scéal breá stairiúil ar leic an dorais againn?

english: What's this, Séamus?
irish: Cén scéal mór é seo a Shéamuis?

english: Hello, Cóilín. Here's the man who'll tell us all about it.
irish: Dia dhuit a Chóilín. Seo é an fear anseo a inseoidh daoibh faoi.

english: -Were you talking to the museum? -What friggin' museum?
irish: -Bhí tú ag caint leis an músaem an raibh? -Cén sórt friggin' músaem?

english: -The museum. -What did they think of your news?
irish: -An músaem. -Agus éist liom a Chóilín céard a cheap siad faoi do scéal?

english: The girl in the office told me that their archaeologist is in Co. Clare.
irish: Bhuel dúirt an cailín liomsa a bhí san oifig, dúirt sí go raibh an seandálaí a bhí acu... ..thíos i gcontae an Chláir i láthair na huaire agus go raibh sí ag iarraidh dul i dteagmháil leis.

english: She said that she would try to contact him.
irish: ..thíos i gcontae an Chláir i láthair na huaire agus go raibh sí ag iarraidh dul i dteagmháil leis.

english: I wonder which archaeologist it is?
irish: Meas tú cén seandálaí a bhí ann?

english: As if you know any of them!
irish: A dhiabhail chomh maith agus go mbeadh aithne agat ar chuile friggin duine acu ann.

english: I think she said Dr. de Brún.
irish: -Feicimse anois gur dhúirt sí an Dr. de Brún. -An Dochtúir Alasdar de Brún ab ea?

english: Dr. Alasdar de Brún?
irish: -Feicimse anois gur dhúirt sí an Dr. de Brún. -An Dochtúir Alasdar de Brún ab ea?

english: I'm not sure.
irish: Bhuel ní bheinn cinnte anois.

english: I bet it's him. He is a lovely man.
irish: Chuirfinn geall gurb é a bhí ann. Fear breá leis an gceart a dhéanamh.

english: He used to come here years ago to learn Irish.
irish: Bhíodh sé thart anseo fadó ag foghlaim Gaeilge.

english: He got an earful from you!
irish: Ar ndóigh fuair sé neart cainte uaitse...

english: You probably couldn't keep your mouth shut then either.
irish: ..mar níl tú in ann do chlab mór a choinneáil dúnta anuas ach an oiread seans.

english: What is there for him around here anyway?
irish: Agus cad é atá le feiceáil aige san áit seo cibé?

english: An important discovery of ancient butter.
irish: Taisce breá tábhachtach sean-ime Angela a fuair Séamus agus iníon an tsairsint sa phortach.

english: Séamus and the sergeant's daughter found it in the bog.
irish: Bhí rud éigin mar sin thuas sa bhaile blianta ó shin fosta.

english: Something like that was found back home years ago.
irish: -Bhí go leor cainte fá dtaobh de ag an am. -Anois a Shéamuis.

english: -There was a lot of talk about it. -See, Séamus?
irish: So beidh tú ag déanamh guided tours do Ros na Rún na Staire amach anseo a Shéamuis an mbeidh?

english: Next you'll be giving guided tours of Ros na Rún's history!
irish: So beidh tú ag déanamh guided tours do Ros na Rún na Staire amach anseo a Shéamuis an mbeidh?

english: As if I don't have enough on my plate!
irish: Nach bhfuil mo dhóthain ar mo friggin' aire. A Bherni tá mé ag iarraidh focal a bheith agam leat.

english: -Berni, I'd like a word with you. -Go on!
irish: -Abair leat. -Go príobháideach más é do thoil é.

english: Privately, please.
irish: -Abair leat. -Go príobháideach más é do thoil é.

english: Right then, excuse me.
irish: Right mar sin. Gabh mo leithscéal.

english: Angela!
irish: Angela!

english: It's sorted. He's looking forward to the party.
irish: Tá sé sin socraithe. Tá sé ag tnúth go mór leis an gcóisir.

english: You're joking!
irish: -Ag magadh atá tú! -Bhuel tá sé ag glacadh leis pé ar bith.

english: -He's fine with it. -Well done.
irish: Bhuel bullaí girsigh.

english: I'll call in tomorrow to discuss everything.
irish: Buailfidh mé isteach amárach agus pléifidh muid an scéal ar fad. Okay?

english: Right, well done.
irish: Right maith thú.

english: It's one thing to say it to me. I'm an adult.
irish: Rud amháin é sin a rá liomsa, is duine fásta mé. Ach rud eile ar fad le dul é a rá le gasúir. Le Evan nach bhfuil ach ceithre bliana d'aois.

english: It's an entirely different matter to say it to a child.
irish: Ach rud eile ar fad le dul é a rá le gasúir. Le Evan nach bhfuil ach ceithre bliana d'aois.

english: Are you telling me how to raise my child?
irish: Fan anois nóiméad. An bhfuil tusa ag iarraidh a bheith ag insint domsa cén chaoi le mo mhac féin a thógáil?

english: No, I'm not. I'm not telling you now and I never will again.
irish: Níl mé a Bherni agus ní bheidh anois ná go brách bíodh a fhios agat.

english: So, what are you trying to tell me?
irish: Bhuel a Mhíchíl céard go díreach atá tú ag iarraidh a rá liom mar sin?

english: I've decided not to have anything to do with Evan in the future.
irish: Bhuel a Bherni séard go díreach atá mé ag iarraidh a rá leat go bhfuil sé socraithe agam...

english: -Micheál! -End of story.
irish: ..gan aon bhaint a bheith agam le Evan as seo amach.

english: You're willing to lie to a little boy and tell him I don't love him.
irish: -Ach a Mhíchíl! -Sin a bhfuil faoi. Má tá tusa sásta dul ag cáitheadh do chuid bréag don leaidín beag ag rá nach bhfuil aon ghrá agamsa dó...

english: After all I've done for him, for both of you.
irish: ..tar éis an méid a rinne mé dó, tar éis an méid a rinne mé don bheirt agaibh.

english: I can't put up with that.
irish: Bhuel níl mise in ann aige sin bíodh a fhios agat.

english: It wasn't like that.
irish: Breathnaigh ní raibh sé mar sin.

english: This is how it is and that's how it's going to be. That's that.
irish: Is mar sin atá sé agus is mar sin a bheidh sé. Agus sin a bhfuil faoi.

english: Don't you realise this is breaking my heart?
irish: An bhfuil tú ag ceapadh nach mbriseann sé seo mo chroí?

english: But you leave me with no alternative.
irish: Ach níor fhág tú an dara rogha agam. Tá trua agam dó.

english: I pity him. I pity him having you as a mother.
irish: Tá trua agam gur tusa an mháthair atá aige mar seisean a bheidh siar le do chuid cneamhaireachta.

english: Your maliciousness will hurt him the most.
irish: Tá trua agam gur tusa an mháthair atá aige mar seisean a bheidh siar le do chuid cneamhaireachta.
Fotheidil le Europus
